def: n. f. A jackdaw.

more: 1. adj. Rose-coloured, rosy, roseate. 2. Name of a horse of Shīrīn the mistress of Farhād.

def: n. m. A cosmetic. pāë gul, phūl kī ḍanḍī , ( Bot .) Flower stalk or flower axis.
adj. (f. -ī), Having full plump cheeks, chubby-cheeked; chubby, plump; handsome; — v.n. To puff out the cheeks (in anger at); to quarrel (with), &c. ( = rūṭhnā).

adj. & s.f. = gulatthī, q.v. adj. Consisting of soft lumps (as boiled rice, khićaṛī, &c.); soft and lumpy; soft; — s.f. Rice and water boiled together and seasoned with salt (generally used in dysentery).
more: s. f. Rice gruel, or rice and water boiled together and seasoned with salt.

def: s. f. Rice boiled to a pap, with the addition of ghī (Qanoon-e-islam ).
s.f. A pill, a bolus; any small globular substance; a lump; — small pox
subs: To take the form of (or to be formed into) a lump; — to grow strong or lusty.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀕𑀮𑀻 (*galī). Cognate with Punjabi ਗਲੀ (galī) / گَلی (galī), Gujarati ગલી (galī), Sindhi ڳَليِ (g̠alyi) / ॻली, Marathi गल्ली (gallī), Bengali গলি (goli).

def: s.m. See s.v. gul. s.m. Noise, din, clamour, confusion of voices, outcry, tumult
more: 1. s. m. A rose garden. 2. The name of a celebrated work written by مصلح الدين شيخ سعدي of Shīrāz.
subs: or g̠ul-gapāṛā, s.m. Clamour, shouts, disturbance, brawl, uproar, &c.
or g̠ul maćānā, v.n. To make a noise, raise a cry or an outcry, to bawl, shout, to make a tumult.
ex:
سارے جہاں سے اچھا ہندوستاں ہمارا
ہم بلبلیں ہیں اس کی، یہ گلستاں ہمارا
sāre jahā̃ se acchā, hindostā̃ hamārā.
ham bulbulẽ ha͠i iskī, yah gulistā̃ hamārā.

Better than the whole world, is our Hindustan;
We are its nightingales, and it is our flower-garden.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُلِسْتَان (gulistān). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi گلستان (glstan /⁠gulistān⁠/). By surface analysis, گُل (gul, “flower”) + ـِسْتَان (-istān).

def: s.f. A knot; conglomeration; twist, entanglement; a tangled web; perplexity; — the bull's-eye (of a target)
more: s. f. Conglomeration, knot. گل جهڙي دلکي guljhaṛī dil kī, Grief, displeasure, mental uneasiness.
subs: (meṅ), To fall into knots, &c.; to become knotted or tangled
(-kī), To take out the knots (of); to open a knot; to disentangle; to unravel
s.f. Mental uneasiness; soreness of heart; grief; displeasure; misunderstanding.

def: n. f. S. गोल a ball, and झटे matted. 1. A knot; a tangled web. 2. Knitting the brow. 3. The bulls eye in a target. guljhaṭī paṛnā , v. n. To be taugled, knotted. guljhaṭī nikālnā , v. a. To unravel; to open a knot. dil kī guljhaṭī , n. m. A misunderstanding; sore.

n. m. Conserve of roses; candied roses. gul kāṭnā, katarnā, tarāshnā, yā lenā , v. a. To snuff a candle. gul-kārī , n. f. Figured work; embroidery. gul katarnā , v. a. 1. To cut out flowers, etc. 2. To do a sharp or smart thing. gul k. , v. a. To put out (a lamp or candle). gul khānā , v. n. To be cauterized; to cauterize one's self. A practice among lovers who burn themselves with a heated coin, ring, etc, as a proff of their love. Gul khāëṅ haiṅ māshūq ke chhallaṅ ke yahāṅ tak Hāthoṅ pe gumāṅ hai mere tāūs ke par kā . I'm with my mistress' ring all seared to that degree A peacock's feathers on my hands you might just see. gul khilānā , v. a. 1. To throw a squib at one. 2. To sow dissension; cause a disturbance. 3. To make a joke at another's expense. Achchhā gul khilāyā ki bhāī bhāīyoṅ meṅ laṛāī ho gaī . 4. See shigūfā khilānā. gul khilnā , v. n. 1. To open (a bud). 2. To turn out or happen (anything wonderful). 3. To be divulged, betrayed. gul-kes ; Sant. kukruchutbahā , n. m. The cockscomb; Amaranthus cruentus , Wat. gul-gasht , n. Walking in the garden; recreation. gul-gīr , n. m. Snuffers. gul-lālā , n. m. The red poppy (In P. tulip). Ek piyālī ke pīte hī ho jāvegā matvālā, Āṅkhoṅ meṅ tere ā-kar khil jāvegā gul lālā . Nazīr. One glass your blood will warm up so, As poppies red your eyes will glow. gul-i-makhmal , n. f. The globe amaranth; Gomphrena globosa , Wat. gul-i-maulsirī , n. A flower; Mimus opselengi. (?) gul-meṅhdī , n. f. The balsam; Impatiens balsamina. gul-mekh . A nail with a large head. gul-i-hazārah , n. The double poppy; Papaver rhœas , Wat. gul h. , v. n. To be extinguished; to go out (a candle). gul-gūṅ , adj. Rosy; ruddy.
s.m. = P گلدم gul-dum, s.f. See s.v. gul.
more: s. m. A stove. گلخن تابي gul-ḵẖan-tāb-ī, s. f. Heating or kindling of a stove.

ety: for orig. گله gula = S. गुड+dim. aff. ūla = S. ल or र
def: s.m. A small ball; a pellet; a marble.
more: n. m. 1. Grain; corn ( anāj ). 2. Sale proceeds of the day. 3. The box in which they are kept. gullah bharnā , v. n. To store up grain. gallah ḍālnā , v. a. To lay by something daily towards an offering. galla-farosh ; H. banyā , n. m. A corn dealer; a grain merchant. gallai' , Rents paid in kind. Carnegy .
n. m. A pellet (shot from a pellet-bow).
(from غلّ) s. m. 1. The produce of the earth. 2. Grain, corn. غله دان g̠ẖalla-dān, s. m. A granary. غله فروش g̠ẖalla-farosh, A grain merchant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غله (ġalla), from Arabic غَلَّة (ḡalla).

def: s.m. A small ball; a pellet (such as is shot from a pellet-bow); a marble.
more: s. m. A pellet shot from a pellet-bow.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *gʷel- (“throat”). Cognate with Latin gula (“throat”), English gullet, and Persian گلو (galu, “throat”).

def: s.f. A vessel used as a till or save-all; a small (hidden) niche in a wall to keep money in (cf. g̠alla).
more: n. f. A small hidden niche in a wall for keeping money.

def: s.f. A span (measure of circumference, i.e. the circle formed by joining the ends of the thumb and fore-finger); — a cob (of Indian corn, &c.); the flower of the keʼoṛā, q.v.; — a honey-comb; — the 'cat,' or short stick in the game of tip-cat (which is struck by the longer one called ḍanḍā); — gullī, s.f. A species of mainā (bird; — in this sense prob. fr. S. guṭikā); — a kind of pigeon; — a whetstone, a polishing stone
more: n. f. H. gol round. 1. Corn-cob; cob. 2. The flower of the keoṛā , q. v. 3. A honey-comb. 4. ( gangā mainā, gang salik, Rām salik ) The bank mainah; acridotheres ginginianus . This is the mainah with grey back, always breeding in large parties in banks, boring very deep holes, eggs pale blue, spread all overIndia. gullī baṅdhnā , v. n. 1. To be formed into a lump. 2. To grow strong or lusty.
subs: or gillī-ḍanḍā, s.m. The game of tip-cat
To play at tip-cat; — to idle away (one's) time
lit. 'To make gillīs'; to argue or debate uselessly.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀕𑀮𑀻 (*galī). Cognate with Punjabi ਗਲੀ (galī) / گَلی (galī), Gujarati ગલી (galī), Sindhi ڳَليِ (g̠alyi) / ॻली, Marathi गल्ली (gallī), Bengali গলি (goli).

def: s.m. A cluster or clump of trees, a thicket; — a bush, a shrub; — a division of an army (consisting of nine platoons, each amounting to nine elephants, nine chariots, twenty-seven horse, and forty-five foot); — the spleen; a chronic enlargement of the spleen, or (as variously situated) any glandular enlargement in the abdomen (as that of the mesenteric gland, &c.), so as to be perceived externally.
s.m. See s.v. gul. s.m. Noise, din, clamour, confusion of voices, outcry, tumult
more: n. m. 1. The pomegranate flower. 2. ( gulnārī raṅg ) Carnation.
gul-i-anār, s. m. 1. The flower of the pomegranate. 2. Scarlet (colour).

def: s. m. The colour of the gul-nār, carnation colour.
s.m. The ankle; — (f. -ā) A person with high ankles.
s.m. Name of a herb which grows in fields sown with ḵẖarīf grains.
s. A sort of fireworks, matches with or without stars ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: or galū; Zend garaṅh, fr. gar; S. गल
def: s.m. The neck, throat, gullet, windpipe; — the voice
more: n. m. See H. galā. gulu-band ; (Europeans) galā-band , n. m. A necktie; cravat. khush-gulū , Having a good or sweet voice.

(or galū ) s. m. The gullet, the throat, the windpipe. گلو گير gulū-gīr, adj. Seizing by the throat; any acrid food or drink that constringes the palate or jaws; being so affected in the throat by trepidation or other cause as to be unable to speak; (figuratively) an accuser. گلو بند gulū-band, s. m. Neckcloth, cravat.
subs: (by Europeans, and native servants, galā-band), s.m. A collar; a scarf for the neck, a neckerchief, neck-tie; a cravat; — a small rectangular plate of gold (studded with gems) worn on the throat

adj. & s.m. Seizing by the throat; seizing or affecting the throat; — any acrid food (or drink) that constringes the palate or jaws; — being so affected in the throat (by trepidation or other cause) as to be unable to speak; one who seizes the throat (of); an accuser.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گلو (gulō). Cognate with Sanskrit गल (gala, “throat, neck”). Doublet of گلا (galā).

def: s.m. The pod of the mahuʼā tree, Bassia latifolia.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گلو (gulō). Cognate with Sanskrit गल (gala, “throat, neck”). Doublet of گلا (galā).

def: s. m. A horseʼs collar ( Harris ).
s.m. A monkey's pouch

def: adj. Of the rose-bower.

ety: old P. gam; fr. Zend gam; S. गम्
def: adj. Lost; wanting; missing; absent; invisible; — wandering, distracted, confounded
more: adj. 1. Missing. 2. Distracted. gum-rāh , adj. 1. Erring; wandering; astray. 2. Fallen away; abandoned. gum-rāh k . To lead astray; mislead; seduce. gum-rāh h. , v. n. 1. To lose one's way; deviate; go astray. 2. To apostatize; become a heretic. gum-rāhī , n. f. 1. Deviation. 2. Apostasy. gum-sum , adj. Still; quiet. gum k. , v. a. 1. To hide. 2. To lose. gum-nām , adj. 1. Unknown to fame. 2. Anonymous. gum ho gayā ; P. gum-shuda , adj. Lost. gum h. , v. n. To be missing or lost.

adj. 1. Lost, wanting, missing. 2. Distracted. گم شده gum-shuda, adj. Lost. گم کرنا gum-karnā, 1. To lose. 2. To be proud. گم کرده gum-karda, adj. Having lost. گم گشته gum-gashta, adj. Lost. گم نام gum-nām, Wanting name, nameless, anonymous, ignoble, inglorious.
subs: adj. & s.m. Losing the way; erring, wandering, astray; deviating; misled, seduced; abandoned, depraved; — one who has lost his way, one who is astray, &c.
v.t. To lose; to lead astray, to mislead, to seduce
s.f. A missing or losing (of) one's way; erring; error; deviation; seduction; — depravity; — apostasy
s.f. The state of being lost or astray; a lost condition
part. Lost; — s.m. One who (or that which) is lost
v.t. To lose; to hide; — v.n. To be proud
adj. Whose name is lost; wanting name, nameless; anonymous; extinct; — unknown to fame; ignoble, inglorious
adv. With the senses lost; senseless; stupefied
To be or become lost or missing, &c.; — gum ho-gayā, part. Lost ( = gum-shuda).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گم (gum).

def: s.m. The plant Pharnaceum mollugo, and its fruit (it is used as a topic, and cure for ear-ache, ague, &c.).
more: n. m. A plant and its fruit, used as a tonic and cure for the ear-ache, ague, etc; Pharnaceum mollugo .
(perhaps from P. گوم) s. m. A name of a plant used as a topick, and cure for the ear-ach, ague, &c. (Pharnaceum mollugo).

def: s. (dakh.) Gadfly, horsefly ( Harris ).

ety: Pehl. gumān; Zend vī-manaṅh; S. वि+मनस्
def: s.m. Doubt, distrust, suspicion; surmise, conjecture; (in comp.) thinking; suspecting (e.g. bad-gumān, q.v.s.v. bad); — opinion, fancy, notion, supposition, imagination; — presumption; probability; — conceit, pride, haughtiness
more: n. m. Z. gumānu . 1. See bharam , (4.) 2. Pride; haughtiness. 1. Ghar meṅ dhān na pān', bībī ko baṛā gumān ! Prov. Of meat and drink forsooth there's scant supply, But, oh! my lady holds herself so high! 2. Talvaryā paṭhān mo se karat gumān . Song. Ṭappā . The soldier with his sword and sash before me cuts a mighty dash. 3. Rakhlan Gaurā je surat ke gumaniyāṅ, Ahe nahīṅ rahlain un ko abhimaniyāṅ re kī . Mag. Jaṭāsārī . Gone is the beauty which was once her pride, How with its loss her lofty airs subside! 4. Kīkar kāṭūṅ, kas karūṅ, aur kas-ke karūṅ kamān, Kheṅchan-vāle chal base, kis par karūṅ gumān? Acacia bark constringent is, but wherefore make it tight, The drawer of the bow is gone, nought left pride to incite. (Women apply this bark to contract the pudendum when it is relaxed). gumān-bharā, gumān-bharī , adj. Puffed-up; proud. Nainā tumhār gumān-bhare, gorī, bolat kāhe nāhīṅ re ? So…
s. m. Opinion, fancy, notion, supposition, imagination, suspicion, doubt, distrust. گمان کرنا gumān karnā, To imagine, fancy, doubt, suspect.
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Full of conceit, puffed-up, proud
or gumān lānā, To think, imagine, suppose, believe, fancy, conjecture; — to doubt; to suspect, to mistrust; — to show pride, be conceited or proud
(-par), To run away with the idea (of); to have suspicion (against)
The supposition, &c. is; it is supposed, &c.; it is expected; it is probable or likely.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُمان (gumān).

def: v.t. To lose; to let (a thing) be lost ( = gaṅwānā).
wety: From گم (gum, “lost, missing”) + آ (ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: adj. & s.m. Suspicious, doubting, distrustful; — jealous; — conceited, vain, proud; — a suspicious character; — a conceited or proud person.
more: Poet. n. m. A proud person (ابهيماني).

ety: perf. part. of gumāshtan, rt. gu-mār, fr. Zend vi+rt. mar (for hmar); S. वि+स्मर्
def: part. & s.m. Deputed, commissioned; appointed; set over; — a superintendent; commissary; deputy; representative; agent, factor; correspondent
more: n. m. گماشتن to appoint. A deputy; representative; agent; correspondent ( kār-pardāz ). gumāshtah-i-qānūngo , n. m. The agent or deputy of the qānūngo, or village registrar and accountant. gumāshtah k . To appoint a manager. gumāshtah-garī , n. f. 1. Agency; office of an agent. 2. Commission.
part. pass. (of گماشتن) Commissioned; s. m. An agent, a factor.
subs: (-ko), To appoint (one) an agent, or manager, &c.
s.f. Commission; agency; office or business of an agent, &c.

Dome-shaped; vaulted. 2. (H. ubhrā ) Convex; in relief. gumbad kī sadā , n. f. 1. An echo from a dome. 2. Return ( badlah , 3).

or gumbaẕ-kī āwāz, s.f. 'Echo of a dome'; the rebound or recoil of one's evil words or deeds on himself; requital, return (syn. badlā).

or gumbaẕ-kī āwāz, s.f. 'Echo of a dome'; the rebound or recoil of one's evil words or deeds on himself; requital, return (syn. badlā).

Sinfulness; criminality; guiltiness. 2. Forfeit; mulct ( tāvāṅ ). 3. Loss; deficit. 4. Revenue derived from judicial fines. gunāh-gārī denā , v. a. To pay a fine, etc. be-gunāh , adj. Innocent; guiltless.…

Without my leave to crush my boddice you must choose! 4. Na gunḍā gāoṅ ko bade, na gāoṅ gunḍe ko . Prov. The profligate has no regard for his village, nor the village for him. 2. A rascal; a rogue.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Dumb, mute, speechless
more: adj. 1. Dumb. 2. Mute; speechless. 1. Gūṅge kā guṛ khaṭṭā na mīṭhā . Prov. A dumb man's sugar is neither sour nor sweet. (He cant tell). 2. Gūṅge ne supnā dekhā, man hī man pachhtāë . Prov. The dumb man has a dream, his mind with keen regrets doth teem. (He cant impart it). gūṅgī pahelī , n. f. A dumb riddle or charade in which signs are used for words. ☞ This used to be a favourite pastime in the seraglio of the king of Dehli. Thus, the finger on the forehead represents karam Fate, and the finger on the cheek points to kallā cheek—the two words together making karam-kallā a cabbage. Similarly, a rap with the knuckle stands for khaṭ (the sound), and one hand rubbed against the other for mal rub, the two together making khaṭ-mal a bug.
subs: s.f. A riddle asked in dumb-show (e.g. a finger placed on the forehead denotes karam, 'fate'; and the finger on the cheek signifies kallā, 'cheek': the two words together make karam-kallā, 'cabbage').

more: s. m. The seed of Abrus precatorius, or the shrub itself. گنجہار gunj-hār, s. m. A necklace composed of the gunj seed.
subs: s.f. A necklace, or a rosary, of gunj-seeds

def: s.f. An echo, a hollow sound; a low murmuring sound, humming, buzzing; — gunj, s.m. (and gunjā, s.f.), The shrub Abrus precatorius, and its seed (which forms the smallest of a jeweller's weights; — syn. gūṅć; and ghuṅćī)
subs: s.f. A necklace, or a rosary, of gunj-seeds

def: s. f. Capacity, room.
more: s. f. Name of an insect. See کنسلاٴي kan-salāʼī.

ety: fr. gunjā, part. of gunjīdan, denom. v. fr P. gunj, 'space, room, capacity'
def: s.f. The power of containing; capacity, room ( = gunjāʼish).
adj. Capable; — profitable, lucrative.
adj. 1. Capable. 2. Profitable.
adj. Thick, dense, close; compact.
s.f. Thickness, denseness, closeness, &c.
v.n. To roar; to growl (cf. gūṅjnā).
more: v. n. To roar, to growl.

def: adj. Buzzing, humming; making a low murmuring sound.

ety: abst. s. fr. gunjā; see gunjāʼī
def: s.f. Holding, containing; room, capacity; room to contain, stowage; — profit; — the revenue capabilities of a village (esp. with reference to a proposed increase of revenue)
more: n. f. 1. Room ( samāī ). 2. Profit (اوت 1.). 3. The revenue capabilities of a village. gunjāyash rakhnā , v. n. To have room for; to bear.
s. f. Capacity, containing, holding, room, profit.
subs: adj. Capable of being held or contained
(-kī), To have room (for), to be capable of holding or containing

def: s.f. (dialec.) Fastening (of a bracelet, &c.), a clasp.
s.f. = gūṅj, 'hum,' &c.
n. f. A clasp. Merī ṭūṭī nathunīāṅ kī gūṅjh, bidesyā se ab na bolūṅ, Rām! E. Wom. song. My nose-ring's clasp he has broken—by Ram I swear I will not speak to this foreigner again.

ety: prob. Prk. गुज्झओ; S. गुह्य+कः, rt. गुह्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Grave, serious; venerable.
s.m. = gūjhā, q.v. s.m. A kind of pastry (made of pounded almonds, pistachio nuts, &c.).
more: s. m. A sort of sweetmeat.

def: s.m. See s.v. gunj. (prob. the P. g̠anj arabicized), s.f.(?) Amorous gesture or behaviour, &c. (see g̠amza).
n. f. A tart.
v.n. = gūṅjnā, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) The shrub Limonia pentaphylla.
s.f. A sparrow.
more: s. f. A sparrow. See کنجشک kunjishk.

def: s.f. An ear-ring.
v.n. = gūṅjnā, q.v.
v.n. To hum, buzz; to sound (as a drum, &c.); to resound; to reverberate, to echo; to roar; to growl.
more: v. n. 1. To resound; echo. 2. To roar.
v. n. To resound, to hum, to buzz, to growl, to roar.

def: Mine of merit; very worthy or skilful.

ety: for A. غنة, v.n. fr. غنّ 'to speak through the nose'
def: s.m. A nasal sound or twang; the nasal n; — buzzing, humming (of flies, &c.); — adj. Nasal (the letter n); quiescent.
more: 1. s. m. A sound through the nose, the buzzing of flies. 2. adj. Quiescent, nasal (the letter ن).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غنه (ġunna), from Arabic غُنَّة (ḡunna).

def: v.t. To praise, laud; — to think upon, consider, judge of; — to put in practice, to practise; — (in Arith.) to multiply (by, -se).
s.f. (rustic) A light whip or scourge (koṛā) of twisted cloth (with which rustic women lash one another at the Holī festival).
more: n. f. A light whip of twisted cloth with which rustic Hin. women lash one another in the Holi festival.
wety: Borrowed from English guinea and Guinea.

def: s.m. Beam (of a ship); cross-timbers (of a boat; — syn. guṛhā or gūṛhā).
more: s. m. A beam (of a ship), the cross timbers (of a boat).

def: s.m. (Dakh.) Spring; fountain; pool; tank, pit.
s.m. (local), Lands assigned rent-free for religious purposes; — a kind of horse bred in Thibet, &c.; a hill-pony.
v.t. prop. guṅthānā, q.v.

v.t. = gūthnā, q.v. v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
more: v. a. S. गुम्फन See guthnā , 1.
v. a. 1. To thread, to string, to plait. 2. To stitch. 3. To spit, or put on a spit.
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).

def: v.t. A dialec. form. of gūṅthnā, or gūthnā, q.v.
v.t. A dialec. form of guṅthwānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to string, &c.; — to cause to be strung; to have or get plaited, &c. (see gūṅthnā).
more: v. a. Caus of گونتهنا q. v.
(causal of گونتهنا gūnthnā ) v. a. To cause to string, &c.

def: adj. (old H.) = untīs, q.v. adj. Twenty-nine.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. guṇ. s.m. Colour; — kind, species; form, mode, fashion (used in comp., e.g. gul-gūṅ, adj. 'Rose-coloured, red')
more: adj. Possessed of talent or virtue; virtuous, accomplished, skilful.
subs: adj. Of different colours; of different sorts or kinds; variegated; various; diversified; motley.

def: adj. See s.v. guṇ. s.m. Colour; — kind, species; form, mode, fashion (used in comp., e.g. gul-gūṅ, adj. 'Rose-coloured, red')
subs: adj. Of different colours; of different sorts or kinds; variegated; various; diversified; motley.

def: m. adj. Skilful.
f. adj. Skilful.
part. adj. & s.m. To be multiplied; — that which is to be multiplied (by), the multiplicand
adj. Hidden, secret; dark, obscure; blind
more: n. The sound in the ear in stupor or fainting.
subs: adj. & adv. Secret; quiet; silent; — in secrecy, or in secret, secretly; quietly; — s.f. Secrecy; silence.
wety: Either onomatopoeic and inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀕𑀧𑁆𑀧- (*gappa-), or inherited from Sanskrit गल्प (galpa). Cognate with Classical Persian گَپ (gap), Punjabi ਗੱਪ (gappa) / گپّ (gappa), Bengali গপ (gop), Gujarati ગપ (gap), Nepali गफ (gaph), Odia ଗପ (gapa), Assamese গপ (gop).

def: s.m. An iron mace; — a throne of iron or of wood.
more: s. 1. An iron mace. 2. A throne of iron or of wood.

def: s.m. corr. of gopāl, q.v. s.m.f. Water; — s.m. Name of a Rishi; — the sacrifice of a cow; — the thunder-bolt; — the sky; heaven, paradise; — the sun; the moon; a member (of the body); — hair (of the body); — s.f. Speech; Sarasvatī, the goddess of speech; — the earth, the world; a mother; — a quarter of the horizon (as east, west, &c.); — the eye; — an arrow; — adj. Got, obtained; — meek, gentle; harmless, inoffensive; simple (e.g. gaʼū brāhmaṇ)
subs: vulg. gavāksh, gawāććh (˚va+ak˚), s.m. 'A bull's-eye'; an air-hole; a loop-hole, a skylight; a window; lattice; — the light admitted by a window
s.m. The killing of a bull or a cow
s.m. (f. -ī), Cow-keeper, cowherd; — ʻa protector of the earth,' a king; — the cowherd Kr̤ishṇa (one of the epithets of Kr̤ishṇ); — a proper name (of men)
s.m. Title of an Upanishad in honour of Kr̤ishṇa
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Having the charge of kine; — protecting the earth; — a cowherd; — a king
s.m. 'The lord of a herd of cows,' a bull
s.m. 'Son of a cow;' a simpleton; a fool
vulg. go-pasu, s.m. A sacrificial cow
vulg. go-pasutā, s.f. The condition of a cow about to be immolated
or gau-thān, s.m. The place where the cattle of a village are assembled; a station for cattle; a cow-pen ( = go-sthān)

adj. & s.m. Ranged or grazed over by cattle; — offering range (or field or scope) for action; within the range (of), accessible, attainable; within the power (of); within sight (of), visible, perceptible; perceived, known; — the range of cattle, pasturage, pasture-ground; range or reach (of the eye, or the understanding, &c.); — the range (or the conjunction or influence) of the planets; — an object of sense (as sound, shape, &c.); — an object of devotion; — perception; information acquired by the perception of the senses
or gau-dān, s.m. The gift of a cow; an offering of a cow
s.m. A cow's tooth; — yellow orpiment; — a white fossil substance (apparently an earthy salt)
s.m. Possession of cows; property in cattle; — a station of cowherds
s.f. 'Dust of the earth'; sunset (the time at which mist seems to rise from the earth); evening, twilight
s.m. Cow's milk; buttermilk; curdled or coagulated milk
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī). Reared on cow's milk; one who is reared on cow's milk, a child; — a sodomite
s.f. A vessel for holding milk, &c.; a milk-pail

s.m., or go-roćanā, s.f. A bright yellow pigment prepared from the urine or the bile of a cow, or vomited in the shape of scibulœ by that animal (it is employed in painting and dyeing, and is of especial use in marking the foreheads of Hindūs with the tilak or sectarial mark; it is also used in medicine as a sedative, tonic, and anthelmintic remedy, &c.)
or gau-sālā, s.f. & m. Cow-shed, cow-house ( = go-śālā)
go-sāṅʼī or go-sāʼīṅ or go-saiṅyā or go-saiyāṅ
s.m. Udder of a cow; — a cluster of blossoms; — a nosegay
s.f. A bunch of grapes; a grape
s.m. A station for cattle; a cow-stall, cow-house, cow-pen (see go-thān)
s.m. The sacrifice of a cow (one of the great sacrifices of the Hindūs in former times; but not permitted in Kali-yug or the present age)
s.m. Master or possessor of cows or of herds; a religious mendicant; — an honorary title affixed to proper names, &c. (see gusāṅʼī)
or gau-śāl, s.m., or go-śālā, s.f. = go-sālā, q.v.

(gosh = gos, gen. of go), s.m. A cow's foot or hoof; — mark or impression of a cow's foot; — puddle or water sufficient to fill a cow's foot-mark; a small puddle; — a measure of capacity (as much as a cow's foot-print would hold)
vulg. goshṭh, s.m. An abode for cattle; cow-house, cow-pen; — any place where animals are kept; — a station of cowherds
s.f. An assembly, a meeting; a society, an association; a confederacy; — family connexions (esp. the dependant or junior branches); — conversation, discourse, dialogue; dispute

s.m. A cow's ear; — a species of deer, Antilope picta (syn. nīl-gāʼo); — a mule; — a kind of serpent; — a span (from the tip of the thumb to that of the ring-finger); — a place of pilgrimage on the Malabar coast (sacred to Śiva); Śiva (as there worshipped)
(for go-karṇī), s.f. The plant Aletris hyacinthoides, and its flower
s.m. The plant Asteracantha longifolia, and Ruellia longifolia, and Tribulus languinosus
s.m. A herd of kine; a multitude of cattle; a cow-station, cow-house; — a village or tract of the Jamna (the country around Vr̤indāvan, the residence of Krishṇ during his youth)
s.m. A measure of distance (being the distance at which the lowing of a cow may be heard)
s.f. The side of a river, or a tank, &c. at which cows drink
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī or -ā), Noxious to kine; — he who or that which kills kine; one who has killed a cow
s.m. 'Cow-heaven'; the heaven of Vishṇu, or of Krishṇa

vulg. gomtī, s.f. Name of a river in Northern India (it falls into the Ganges between Banāras and Gāzīpūr); — name of a Vedic hymn (a prayer or formula to be repeated during expiation for killing a cow); — cow-dung
or gau-mukh, s.m. A kind of musical instrument (a sort of horn or trumpet); — a hole in a wall (of a peculiar shape), made by thieves, &c.; a breach (syn. seṅdh)

or gau-mukhī, s.f. The chasm in the Himālaya mountains through which the Ganges flows (erroneously conceived to be shaped like a cow's mouth); — a cloth bag containing a rosary (the beads of which are counted by the hand thrust inside)
or gau-mutr, s.m. Cow's urine (which has to be imbibed in certain kinds of penance)

or go-medak, s.m. A gem or precious stone brought from the Himālaya and the Indus (described as of four sorts, — white, pale yellow, red, and dark blue); a kind of yellowish or tawny gem, a topaz; — go-meda-sannibha, s.m. Chalcedony or opal
s.m. The offering or sacrifice of a cow
s.m. (lit. 'having a nose like that of an ox'), A large kind of serpent, a boa-constrictor

s.m. lit. 'Nourisher of kine'; name of a celebrated hill in Vrindāvan, about fifteen miles to the west of Mathurā (it is the Parnassus of the Hindūs; and is said to have been lifted up and supported by Krishṇa upon one finger for seven days, to shelter the cowherds from a storm of rain sent by Indra to test Krishṇa's divinity; — hence Krishṇ is called Govardhan-dhar, and Giri-dhar, 'the hill-supporter')
s.m. 'Lord of Govardhan,' a name of Krishṇa

adj. & s.m. Obtaining cattle; finding cattle or cows; — a cow-keeper, chief herdsman, one of the most usual appellations of Krishṇa in that form; — a proper name (of a man); name of a mountain; — govind-rām, s.m. A proper name (of a man)
s.f. The killing a cow (a most heinous crime among Hindūs)
or gavīś (˚va+īśa), s.m. (f. -ā), An owner of kine.

def: s. (dakh.) Belabouring, kicking and cuffing ( Harris ).
s.f.(?) (colloq.) A boys' game ( = gup-ćup).
n. f. S. गुपचुप 1. Silence. 2. ( gup-chavval ) Boys' game. 3. A sweetmeat. Gup chup kī miṭhāī, jis ne khāī us ne gāī . Delhi Sweetmeat sellers' cry.
s.m. (f.?) A boys' game (in which one puffs out his cheeks, and another strikes them with both hands so as to produce a sound); — a kind of sweetmeat made of curds.
s.f. A cave, cavern; den; vault; — a retreat, hiding-place; a thicket
more: n. f. S. गुहा 1. A cave; den. 2. A thicket. guphā meṅ baiṭhnā , v. n. To lead a hermit's life; retire from the world.
subs: To settle down in a cave or cell, to lead the life of a hermit.

def: s.m. A tassel; a wreath; a bunch (of flowers, &c.).
more: n. m. 1. A tassel. 2. A bunch of flowers.

def: part. adj. (f. -ā), & adv. Protected, guarded, preserved; — hidden, concealed, kept secret; secret; withdrawn from sight, invisible; (in Gram.) not expressed, understood; — secretly, privately; — s.m. An appellation (of frequent use) forming the last member of compound proper names of men of the Vaiśya or third caste
more: adj. 1. Hidden ( poshīdah ). 2. Invisible. 3. ( Gram .) Understood (an ellipsis). gupt , adv. Secretly; privately. gupt-āmdanī , n. f. Income from a hidden source, as bribes, etc. gupt-dān , n. m. A gift made in secret. As when a person leaves property with a Brahman and never reclaims it, or presents him with a sealed bag, or drops a present in a sacred pool of water at Kurakshetra, in which the pilgrim has bathed at a solar eclipse, leaving the officiating Brahmans to search for it. gupt k. , v. a. To hide; conceal. gupt-mār , n. f. 1. Hurting so as to leave no marks. 2. Taunts; gibes; sneers. gupt māl , n. m. Hidden wealth or treasure. gupt h., gupt rahnā , v. n. To be invisible.
subs: s.f. Income from a hidden or secret source (as bribes, &c.)

s.m. A secret gift or present, a hidden donation; a valuable thing given in a concealed manner to Brāhmans or to a deity (as when a person leaves property with a Brāhman and never reclaims it; or presents him with a sealed bag; or drops a present in a sacred pool in which he has bathed at a solar eclipse, leaving the officiating Brāhmans to search for it)
v.n. To remain hidden, lie concealed; to be or continue invisible
s.f. A beating or hurt that leaves no marks; — taunt, gibe, sneer
s.m. A disguise, or dress, &c., used for concealment; — a false or deceitful person; a hypocrite, dissembler; an actor, a mimic (syn. ćhalī, kaptī; bahu-rūpī)
v.n. To be hidden or concealed; to be invisible.
wety: From Sanskrit गुप्त (gupta).

def: s.f. Protecting, protection; concealing, concealment; a means of protection.
more: n. f. A sword-stick. guptī chalānā , v. a. To stab secretly.
s. f. (dakh.) A foil to fence with, a small sword, a sword stick. گپتي چلانا guptī chalānā, To fence with swords ( Harris ).

def: s.f. A hidden sword; a sword-stick.
s.m. = گرو guru, q.v.
more: n. m. S. गुरु A rule of Arithmetic; a short method; a formula.

n. m. S. गुड Molasses; treacle; the produce of the first inspissation of the juice of the cane. 1. Chorī kā guṛ mīṭhā . Prov. Stolen treacle is sweet. (Stolen kisses are the sweetest). 2. Jahāṅ guṛ hogā vahāṅ makkhiyāṅ bhinkeṅgī . Prov. Where treacle is the flies will buzz. 3. Guṛ khāeṅ, gulguloṅ se parhez! Prov. Sugar he eats, sweet cakes he shuns! (Inconsistency). 4. Guṛ dīye mare to zahar kyoṅ dīje? Prov. If sugar will kill why give poison? 5. Guṛ na de to guṛ kī sī bāt to kahe . Prov. If sweets you cannot give sweet your discourse might be. 6. Thailī meṅ rupaiyā yā muṅh meṅ guṛ . Prov. Or money in your bag, or sweetness on your tongue. 7. Gāṅḍe se ganḍerī piyārī. guṛ se piyārā gāṅḍā, Mā bahan se jorā piyārī jis se hoë gujārā . Wom. Dearer than sugarcane the joint that's sown, than sugar sugarcane, Than mother, sister dear the wife who shares your joy and pain. 8. Achchhī bhaī! guṛ sa…
adj. or s. m. See گرو guru.
subs: (fem. gurvi, or gurviṇī), Heavy, weighty; great, large, extended, long (as a vowel), grave (as an accent); difficult, hard; high (in degree), vehement, violent, excessive, deep, much; important, serious, momentous; valuable, highly prized, dear, beloved; venerable, honourable, respectable; excellent; best; — one who is an object of reverence, a venerable or respectable person (as a father, mother, &c.); a spiritual parent or preceptor (from whom a youth receives the initiatory Mantra or prayer and who conducts the necessary ceremonies up to the period of investiture with the sacrificial thread); a spiritual guide, a religious teacher (one who explains the law and religion to his pupil, instructs him in the Śāstras, &c.); a pastor; a teacher (generally); a superior; — a name of Brihaspati or the planet Jupiter (who is considered as the guru or preceptor of the gods); — a wise man, a sage ( = gur-gyānī); — (ironic.) a consummate knave
vulg. gur-bhāʼī, s.m. Follower of the same spiritual preceptor or guide, fellow-sectarian, fellow-disciple; fellow-student
s.m. (f. -ī), The child (natural or adopted) of a religious preceptor

vulg. gur-jan, s.m. pl. Religious or venerable persons, eminent persons, great people, respectable people; — s.m. sing. A venerable person (as a father, a religious preceptor, &c.), an elder, a superior
vulg. gur-daććhanā, s.f. A fee given to a spiritual preceptor; rent-free land given to a religious teacher
s.m. The pronouncing a blessing on a religious guide
s.m. A respectful epithet of any guru
s.m. The eighth Nakshatra or lunar constellation Pushya (of which Brihaspati is the ruling deity)
s.m. A serious or momentous affair; — the business or office of a spiritual teacher
s.m. 'A guru with bells' (hung round his neck), a thorough knave
vulg. guru-laćh, or gur-laćh, s.m. = next
adj. (f. -ā), Heavy, hard of digestion; — s.m. Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only
s.f. The wife of a spiritual teacher
(or gur-) mukh honā, v.n. To receive the initiatory Mantra from a guru; to become a disciple or pupil
s.f. Name of the character in which the Panjābī language is written
vulg. gur-bār, s.m. 'The day of Jupiter,' Thursday.

def: s.m. Raw or coarse sugar (the produce of the first inspissation of the juice of the sugar-cane); molasses, treacle
more: s. m. Molasses, treacle, raw sugar. گڙانبه guṛāmba (Sans. गुडाम्र ) Mangos boiled with meal and sugar, resembling mango fool.
subs: (S. guḍa+āmra), s.m. Mangoes boiled with meal and sugar (resembling mango-fool)
or guṛ-phūl, s.m. The plant Bassia latifolia (see mahuʼā)
Anything to be said or done clandestinely or in secret; a secret or underhand business or work to be going on.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi गुड (guḍa), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀼𑀟 (guḍa), from Sanskrit गुड (guḍá). Cognate to Punjabi ਗੁੜ (guṛ), Gujarati ગોળ (goḷ), Marathi गूळ (gūḷ), Bengali গুড় (guṛ).

def: s.m. Unripe grapes or dates; — a colour in pigeons.
more: s. m. 1. Unripe grapes or dates. 2. A colour in pigeons.

ety: v.n. fr. غرب 'to go away, pass away.' &c.
def: s.m. A crow; raven; rook; jackdaw; — a kind of (Arab) boat or vessel (vulg. called a 'grab').
more: s. m. A crow; a kind of ship or sailing vessel, (vulg.) a grab.
s. m. A fly boat ( Harris ).

def: n. m. 1. Turning up the soil. 2. Deep weeding.
s.m. Weeding; digging up; breaking up and pulverizing the ground in a field; — the process of ploughing through a field of bājrā, &c. when the plant is about a foot high.
s.m. pl. (of g̠arīb), Strangers; the poor.
more: s. plur. (of غريب) Strangers the poor.

def: v.t. To dig, dig up; to weed.
s.m.(?) Name of a medicine, Menispermum glabrum or cordifolium (the infusion of which is used as a febrifuge).
more: s. m. Name of a medicine. (Menispermum glabrum).

ety: gorā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Fairness, whiteness, fair complexion; yellowness; pale redness, ruddiness (syn. gorā-pan).
s. f. Fairness, whiteness, yellowness, pale redness.
s.f. Growl; snarl.
ex: شیر کی گرج سنو
šēr kī garaj suno!
listen to the roar of the lion!
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गर्जन (garjana).

ety: prob. a dialec. var. of garajnā
def: v.n. To snarl; to growl (syn. ghuraknā).
wety: Adapted from Sanskrit गर्जति (gárjati). Doublet of گَاجْنَا (gājnā). Compare Maithili गरजब (garᵊjab) / 𑒑𑒩𑒖𑒥 (garajaba), Punjabi ਗਰਜਣਾ (garjaṇā) / گَرجݨا (garjṇā), Gujarati ગરજવું (garajvũ), Marathi गरजणे (garajṇe).

r: g̠uraknā, g̠urkānā, g̠urkī, ghurkānā, ghuraknā, ghurkī
def: v.n. = H غرکانا g̠urkānā, v.t. = H غرکي g̠urkī, s.f. (local) for ghurkānā, ghuraknā, and ghurkī respectively, qq.v.

v.t. To cause to be dug, to have or get (a hole, &c.) dug or excavated; — to cause to dig; — to dig ( = goṛnā).
more: v. a. 1. To dig. 2. (caus. of گوڙنا) To cause to dig.

more: (part. act. of غريدن) adj. Roaring, making a loud noise, crying out.

def: s.m. Wild boar, boar, hog.

ety: for orig. گراز gurāz; fr. Zend vi+rāz = S. वि+राज्
def: adj. Pompous, haughty; frowning; bold, brave.
more: (from غرز) adj. Frowning, haughty ( Hunter ), bold (?).

ety: g̠urāz, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Pompousness, haughtiness; frowning; boldness, bravery.
s.m. A cat
more: s. m. A cat. گربهٴمسکين gurba-e-miskīn, adj. A wicked rogue.
subs: A meek cat; (fig.) a wicked rogue.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گربه (gurba).

def: s.m. corr. of غربا gurabā, q.v. s.m. pl. (of g̠arīb), Strangers; the poor.

ety: inf. n. of غرب 'to become a stranger or foreigner, to go to a foreign country'
def: s.f. Travelling (to foreign countries), going abroad; emigration; — being far from (one's) home or native country; the state or condition of a stranger, or foreigner, or exile; wretchedness, misery; humility, lowliness
more: s. f. 1. Travelling, emigration, being far from oneʼs countrymen and friends. 2. Wretchedness. غربت زده g̠ẖurbat-zada, Oppressed with wretchedness.
subs: adj. Oppressed with misery or wretchedness, wretched, miserable.

def: s.m. See s.v. گرو guru. adj. & s.m. (fem. gurvi, or gurviṇī), Heavy, weighty; great, large, extended, long (as a vowel), grave (as an accent); difficult, hard; high (in degree), vehement, violent, excessive, deep, much; important, serious, momentous; valuable, highly prized, dear, beloved; venerable, honourable, respectable; excellent; best; — one who is an object of reverence, a venerable or respectable person (as a father, mother, &c.); a spiritual parent or preceptor (from whom a youth receives the initiatory Mantra or prayer and who conducts the necessary ceremonies up to the period of investiture with the sacrificial thread); a spiritual guide, a religious teacher (one who explains the law and religion to his pupil, instructs him in the Śāstras, &c.); a pastor; a teacher (generally); a superior; — a name of Brihaspati or the planet Jupiter (who is considered as the guru or preceptor of the gods); — a wise man, a sage ( = gur-gyānī); — (ironic.) a consummate knave
subs: vulg. gur-bhāʼī, s.m. Follower of the same spiritual preceptor or guide, fellow-sectarian, fellow-disciple; fellow-student
s.m. (f. -ī), The child (natural or adopted) of a religious preceptor

vulg. gur-jan, s.m. pl. Religious or venerable persons, eminent persons, great people, respectable people; — s.m. sing. A venerable person (as a father, a religious preceptor, &c.), an elder, a superior
vulg. gur-daććhanā, s.f. A fee given to a spiritual preceptor; rent-free land given to a religious teacher
s.m. The pronouncing a blessing on a religious guide
s.m. A respectful epithet of any guru
s.m. The eighth Nakshatra or lunar constellation Pushya (of which Brihaspati is the ruling deity)
s.m. A serious or momentous affair; — the business or office of a spiritual teacher
s.m. 'A guru with bells' (hung round his neck), a thorough knave
vulg. guru-laćh, or gur-laćh, s.m. = next
adj. (f. -ā), Heavy, hard of digestion; — s.m. Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only
s.f. The wife of a spiritual teacher
(or gur-) mukh honā, v.n. To receive the initiatory Mantra from a guru; to become a disciple or pupil
s.f. Name of the character in which the Panjābī language is written
vulg. gur-bār, s.m. 'The day of Jupiter,' Thursday.

def: s.f. The creeping-plant Menispermum glabrum.
more: n. f. S. गडूची A creeping plant; menispermum glabrum , Wat.
P.; n. m. S. गुडची. A medicine; Menispermum glabrum or cordifolium , Wat. The infusion is used as a febrifuge.
s. f. Name of a creeping plant. (Menispermum glabrum).

ety: Pehl. gūrt˚; Zend vareda, fr. vared; S. वर्ध, rt. वर्ध्
def: adj. Strong, brave, valiant, valorous, warlike.

ety: prob. S. गुड+द+कः, rt. दा 'to cut,' &c.

s.m. A kidney; the reins; — courage
more: s. m. 1. A kidney. 2. Courage. ¶ girda, 1. A shield. 2. A round pillow. 3. A round flat loaf or cake of bread.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُرْدَه (gurda).

def: s.f. corr. of guru-dakshiṇā, q.v.s.v. گرو guru.
s. (dakh.) A sort of sweetmeat ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: rt. of gureḵẖtan = prep. gu = Zend vi = S. वि+reḵẖtan (rt. rez), fr. Zend rt. rić = S. रिच्
def: part. adj. Flying, fleeing, fugitive (used as last member of compounds); — s.f. Flying (from); flight, escape; avoiding, shunning; a diversion, digression; deviation; regression; abstaining (from), abstinence; fasting; aversion, dislike, abhorrence
more: n. f. گريختن to fly. 1. Escape flight. 2. ( Rhet .) A diversion; digression. gurez k. , v. a. To shun; avoid; evade. insāf se gurez k. , v. a. To evade justice.

(from گريختن) 1. s. f. Flight, deviation, digression, abhorrence, aversion, dislike, abstinence, fasting, regression. 2. part. act. (in compos.) Fugitive, fleeing. گريزپا gurez-pā, adj. Fugitive, runaway, fleeing.
subs: (-se), To fly (from); to avoid, shun; to evade (e.g. inṣāf-se gurez karnā); — to abstain (from); to be averse (to), &c.
s.f. Time or place of fleeing; the place (or occasion) in which it is proper to avoid or fly (from a person or thing); place of escape; place to fly to, place of refuge, asylum, retreat.

ety: imperf. part. of gureḵẖtan; see gurez
def: part. Taking flight, fleeing, fugitive.

ety: for A. غرفة, v.n. fr. غرف 'to take out'; 'to cut,' &c.
def: s.m. An upper chamber or apartment; a hall, a parlour; — a window.
more: n. m. 1. A window ( khiṛkī ). 2. ( Slang ) A hidden place; a corner. gurfe kī bāt chīt, Slang . A secret conversation.

def: s.f. Reproof, chiding, threatening, threat, intimidation, bullying (as of a coward who affects bravery), bluster
more: n. f. See chīṅ paṛākh . Threat; intimidation; bullying. Mujh se gurfish na lānā!
subs: (-se), To practise or try intimidation or bullying (with or on).

ety: Pehl. gurg or vurg; Zend vehrka; S. वृक
def: s.m. A wolf
subs: s.m. A wolf's whelp; a young wolf.

more: n. m. 1. A scullion. 2. A dependant menial. 3. A knee joint. (?)
s. m. 1. A servant boy, brat. 2. Vagabond.

def: s.m. Knee-joint.
s.m. = گرگا gurgā, q.v
more: s. (dakh.) The knee: see زانو zānū. گڙگي پو منڐي جهکانا guṛge po munḍī jhukānā (dakh. i. q. فکر کرنا) To think, meditate, consider, reflect (?).

def: s.f. A kind of shoe or slipper.
more: n. f. A kind of shoe.
s. f. A kind of shoe or slipper.

def: s.m. (dialec.) The knee-joint ( = gurgā, q.v.). s.m. A scullion, a menial servant, servant boy (syn. bāsan-mājhā; ṭahluʼā); a dependent menial; — a low fellow, a vagabond.
s.f. A dialec. form of the next, q.v.
s.f. Knee-breeches, short drawers (cf. guṛgī).
more: s. (dakh.) Breeches, short drawers ( Harris ): see گڙگي guṛgī.

def: s.f. = گرگي gurgī, q.v. s.f. Knee-breeches, short drawers (cf. guṛgī).
more: s. (dakh.) Breeches or short drawers, reaching below the knee and fitting tight.

def: s.f. A menial servant; a wench; a slave-girl.

ety: by redupl. of g̠ur = Zend g̠ar = S. गर् (गॄ)
def: adj. Growling; grumbling; muttering with rage; — ruptured; — s.f. Growling; raging; noise, uproar, tumult.
s.f. The cold fit of a fever, ague, quotidian fever (cf. guṛguṛī).
more: n. f. A small hukkā , q. v. Maṅgāo phūlon kā gahnā, ilāchiyāṅ, aur lauṅg! Maṅgāo guṛguṛī korī bhī, aur ek chilam! Wom. (For an offering). Rangīṅ.
s. f. Ague, the cold fit of a fever, a quotidian fever.

def: s.f. A small ḥuqqa, a kind of pipe for smoking.
more: s. f. A small ḥuḳḳa, a kind of pipe for smoking.

def: s. (dakh.) A gipsy ( Harris ).

adj. S. गूढ 1. Abstruse; difficult. 2. Hidden; obscure; secret; enigmatical; mysterious. gūṛh-arth , Difficult to understand. gūṛh-baktā , n. m. One who uses big words; a pedant. gūṛh-chār , n. m. A spy.
s.m. Beam, cross-timber (of a ship or boat).
s.m. = guṛhā, and gūṅṛā, qq.v. s.m. Beam, cross-timber (of a ship or boat).
part. Covered, veiled, hidden, concealed; secret, private; disguised; — difficult, abstruse, occult, obscure; abstract; mysterious, enigmatical
more: adj. 1. Abstruse, obscure, difficult, abstracted. 2. Secret, mysterious, enigmatical. گوڙهہ بکتا gūṛh-baktā ( गूढवक्ता ) A pedagogue, a pedant.
subs: (˚ha+ar˚), Of obscure or hidden meaning; of abstruse import
or gūṛh-vaktā, s.m. (lit. 'saying profound things'), A pedagogue; a pedant
s.m. A secret emissary; a disguised agent; a spy
vulg. gūṛhaj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Born privately or secretly; — the son of a concealed birth; a son born secretly of a woman whose husband is absent, the real father being unknown
s.m. A secret emissary; a spy
s.m. A secret way; a subterraneous passage; a bye-road

def: s.m. 'The shoe-flower,' the plant Hibiscus syriacus.

Well-beaten or threshed.

s.m. = H گوڙهتا गूढ़ता gūṛhatā, s.f. (S. gūḍhatā; gūḍhatva), Concealedness; concealment; secrecy.
qq.v.

ety: prob. govar or gobar, q.v.+S. प्रकरः
def: s.m. A heap or stack of cow-dung.
s.f. A glass bead; bead or stone (of a rosary, or necklace, &c.); — vertebra, a joint (of the spine).
more: n. f. S. गुटिका 1. A bead (of a rosary, etc.). 2. Vertebra; a joint of the spine.

n. f. S. कुरूण्टी 1. A doll. 1. Damṛī kī guṛyā, ṭakā ḍolī kā! Prov. For the doll but a farthing you pay, and a penny to fetch her away! 2. Shādī hai, kuchh guṛyoṅ kā byāh thoṛā hī hai! Prov. Saying. A marriage feast is not a doll's wedding. (It is an expensive affair). 3. Tulsī! pratimā pūjibo jiyoṅ guṛyoṅ kā khel, Bheṭ bhai jab pīū se dhare piṭārī mel . Tulsī Dās. Tulsī! idol-worship is doll's play, Found a husband dolls are put away. 2. A humble or poor bride. Guṛyā saṅvār dūṅgī, arī, bhīk māṅg kar . Wom. Jān Sāhab. guṛyoṅ kā ālā , m. ( lit . niche). A doll's house; a baby house. guṛyoṅ kā biyāh , n. m. 1. Marriage between two dolls. 2. A poor man's wedding. guṛyoṅ kā khel , n. m. 1. Playing with dolls. 2. An easy matter.
s. f. A bead (of a rosary, &c.).

ety: guṛ, q.v. + iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. A seller of guṛ or raw sugar.
more: s. m. One whose business is to sell گڙ guṛ, q. v.
ex:
Kabira [encore], Yeh Jawāni Hai Dīwānī
... گُڑیا رے گُڑیا تیرا گُڈّا پَردیسِیا جوڑی آسمنی ہوگئی شَگُن پے دیکھو شادمانی ہوگئی ...
... guṛyā re guṛyā terā guḍḍā pardesiyā joṛī āsmnī hogī śagun pe dekho śādmānī hogī ...
Oh doll oh doll, your male-doll is a foreigner. Your match/pairing has become heavenly. On the omen, look happiness has become.

def: s.f. = guṛiyā, q.v. s.f. A glass bead; bead or stone (of a rosary, or necklace, &c.); — vertebra, a joint (of the spine).

... guṛyā re guṛyā terā guḍḍā pardesiyā joṛī āsmnī hogī śagun pe dekho śādmānī hogī ...

def: n. ( Mech .) Rose-bit.
s.m. The country of Guzerat (in western India); — the people of Guzerat; — a native of Guzerat.
s.f. Name of a Rāgiṇī or musical mode.
s.m. A Georgian; — a servant-boy; — a hut; — a kind of dog.
more: n. m. 1. A Georgian. 2. A servant boy. 3. A hut. 4. A dog. Agar rātoṅ ko āūṅ to mujhe sarkār kā gurjī Idhar se ān-kar chimṭe, udhar se pāsbāṅ lipte . Inshā If in the night I come the dog pins me this side, On that I'm in the burly watchman's grasp beside.
s. m. 1. A Georgian. 2. A servant boy. 3. A hut. 4. A kind of dog.

ety: Pārsī kirūh or kiroh?; Zend kharedha; cf. S. वर्ग
def: s.m.(f.?) A collection or party (of men), company, band, troop, crew, gang, group; people, levy of people; a class, an order.
more: n. m. See جماعت (1, 2, 3, 5). 1. A collection or party of men; a gang. 2. A class; order. giroh-i-shurakā , n. A community of sharers. ādmīyoṅ kā giroh . G. G. A mob. ḍākuoṅ kā giroh . G. G. A gang of dacoits.
s. m. f. A band, troop, people levy of people, company, crew, sect.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُروه (gurōh), from Middle Persian 𐭢𐭥𐭥𐭤 (glwh’ /⁠grōh⁠/, “group, crowd”). Compare Sindhi گِروهَه (girohah).

def: s.m. = غره g̠urra, q.v.

ety: for A. غرة g̠urrat, inf. n. of غرّ 'to have whiteness, or a white patch, &c. on the forehead' (a horse
def: s.m. Whiteness; clearnness, brightness; — a white mark, a star, or blaze on the forehead of a horse; — a speckled, or iron-grey, or roan horse; — the first, or commencement (of the month, &c.), the night in which the new moon is seen, the first day of the moon, calends; — the first appearance or dawn of day, morning or its splendour; — the face, aspect, forehead, front (of a man); — the first, the best, or the chiefest (of anything); — g̠urra batānā, or g̠urra denā (-ko), To put (one) off to the (next) new moon; to put off, to shuffle with, to get rid of, &c.; to pass over or by (syn. ṭāl-denā).

ety: g̠ur˚, prob. = S. गर् (गॄ); but cf. S. घुर् cf. also P. g̠urrīdan, whence g̠urrish: — rt. Zend g̠ar = S. गर्
def: v.n. To growl; to roar; to purr (as a cat); to frown, look menacing.
more: v. n. 1. To growl; roar. 1. Khānā! aur gurrānā . Prov. Feed and growl! 2. Jiskā khāë, usī pe gurrāë? Mah. Wom. Growl at the hand that feeds you? 2. To purr as a cat.

def: s.f. = غره g̠urra, q.v.
s.f. Parched barley.

more: n. m. guryānī , n. f. 1. A religious teacher; spiritual guide. 1. Gur gur hī rahe, chele chīnī ho gaë . Prov. The priest treacle (a priest) remains, his pupil sugar's rank attains (high secular office). 2. Gurū sharan gati āë-ke jo sumre Siyā Rām, Yahāṅ rahe ānaṅd se, aṅt base Haridhām . Who at priest's feet cry 'Sītā Ram' continually, Joy's theirs where'er they be, their god reached finally. 3. Pūjā gur kī kījye, sab pūjā jeṅhi māṅhi, Jab jal sīṅche mūl meṅ, sakhā, patr aghāiṅ . The worship of your priest all worship comprehends, As water at the root to all the leaves extends. 4. Gur bin mile na gyān, bhāg bin mile na sampat . Prov. Without a teacher knowledge you cant get, without good luck no riches you can get. 2. A teacher. Gurū bin byākul chelvā, kaṅṭh bin bāur gīt . Bhoj. Prov. Disciple restless priest without, as song sounds harsh good voice without. 3. ( gur gyānī ) A wise man; …

(or gur-) mukh honā, v.n. To receive the initiatory Mantra from a guru; to become a disciple or pupil
s.f. Name of the character in which the Panjābī language is written
vulg. gur-bār, s.m. 'The day of Jupiter,' Thursday.
wety: From Sanskrit गुरु (guru).

def: s.m. = guru-laksh, q.v.s.v. guru.
more: 1. s. m. A saint, a holy man, a spiritual director. گورو لچهہ gūrū-lachh ( गुरुलक्ष्य ) Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only. 2. adj. Heavy, hard of digestion.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गौरव (gaurava, “cumbrousness, importance”); see गौर (gaura, “shining, brilliant”) for more.

def: s.f. The office, or profession, or condition of a guru or spiritual preceptor.
s.f. The wife of a spiritual preceptor; a religious preceptress.
s.f. = guruʼāʼin, q.v. s.f. The wife of a spiritual preceptor; a religious preceptress.

ety: inf. n. of غرب 'to be hidden'; 'to set,' &c.
def: s.m. Setting (of the sun, &c.); occultation; — sunset; — the west
more: n. m. 1. Setting (of the sun, etc.); occultation (H. ast , 1). 2. Sunset.
s. m. 1. Setting (of the sun, &c.). 2. The west. غروب هونا g̠ẖurūb honā, To set (the sun or moon, &c.).
subs: v.n. To set (the sun, or moon, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غروب (ġurūb), from Arabic غُرُوب (ḡurūb).

def: s. m. Thursday.
s.m. (orig.) 'A thing by which one is deceived'; pride, haughtiness, vanity, vainglory
more: n. m. Pride (ابهمان). Tagāful, hayā, nāz, shokhī, gurūr, Har ek apne mauqe se vaqt-i-zurūr . Mīr Hasan. Indifferent, modest, loving, saucy, proud, Each pretty air upon occasion showed. gurūr k. , v. a. To be proud ( dimāg k .).
(from غرّ) s. m. Pride, haughtiness, vain glory, vanity.
subs: v.n. To practise haughtiness, to be proud, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Persian غرور, from Arabic غُرُور (ḡurūr).

def: s.f. = g̠urūr.
v.n. (dialec.) To cry, stopping the breath; to sob.
more: v. a. S. क्रुश. To sob. (?)

def: adj. Hungry.
s.f. Hunger.
s.f. Weight, heaviness; burden, trouble; weightiness, gravity, importance, dignity; — the position or office of a (religious) teacher, tutorship.
